title: Functions should not be nested too deeply
description: Functions should not be nested too deeply. Analyze your code adaptability-focused maintainability Code Smell brain-overload. Why is this an issue? More Info: Nested functions refer to the practice of defining a function within another function. These inner functions have access to the variables and parameters of the outer function, creating a closure. While nesting functions is a common practice in JavaScript, deeply nested functions can make the code harder to read and understand, especially if the functions are long or if there are many levels of nesting. This can make it difficult for other developers or even yourself to understand and maintain the code.
recommendation: Extract deeply nested functions to separate declarations to improve code readability and maintainability.

checks.message: Function declaration is nested 5+ levels deep. Extract inner functions to separate declarations for better readability and maintainability.
Function is nested at the 6th level. This significantly impacts code readability - consider extracting nested functions.
Function nesting has reached 6 levels deep. Refactor to extract inner functions and reduce complexity.
